compositional_truth_check.reconciliation_notes: P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P3 (Pothos) is present with the rabbit anchor. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are present in the black pot, though the specimen is showing signs of severe decline.

inventory_reconciliation.systemic_losses: None confirmed, but P2/P4 are in critical condition.
inventory_reconciliation.new_introductions: Eggshell fragments observed in P3 and P2/P4 soil surface.

plant_audit.p2_p4: High stress. The central stem has lost turgor and is leaning significantly. Leaf tissue appears chlorotic.
plant_audit.p3: Stable. The white rabbit anchor remains in situ.

biome_observations.surface_debris: Significant eggshell fragmentation noted in both black pots, likely an intentional soil amendment or pest deterrent.

temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-2: Progressive postural collapse of the P2/P4 specimen; leaf drooping is pronounced.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: The P2/P4 specimen has reached a state of near-total collapse. The P3 Pothos remains static.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently experiencing a bifurcated health trajectory. While P1 and P3 remain stable, the specimen in the black pot (P2/P4) is undergoing rapid senescence or physiological failure, evidenced by the loss of structural rigidity and drooping. The introduction of eggshells suggests an attempt to remediate soil pH or calcium levels, but the plant has not responded positively as of the current timestamp.
